jokes.text: Oh, didn't I tell you?
jokes.setup: Someone asks where Hilary is taking things
jokes.punchline: Hilary's casual, oblivious response suggesting she forgot to mention something major
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary self-absorption - casually forgetting to mention major life decisions

jokes.text: The other day when I went out to buy that sweater... I bought a house to match. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Normal explanation about buying a sweater
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals she bought an entire house to match the sweater
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - only Hilary would buy a house to match clothing. Absurd logic delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Do I know how to accessorize or what?
jokes.setup: Just revealed buying a house to match a sweater
jokes.punchline: Treating a house purchase as an accessory decision
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity by framing real estate as fashion accessorizing

jokes.text: The hell she can't.
jokes.setup: Someone says Hilary can't take all that stuff
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately defending Hilary's excessive behavior
jokes.explanation: Phil's enabling of Hilary's materialism, economically delivered

jokes.text: Carlton, that's Beverly Hills. Yeah, the bad part. 90211.
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about standing alone somewhere dangerous
jokes.punchline: It's Beverly Hills, with Carlton seriously calling it 'the bad part' and citing the ultra-wealthy zip code
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Carlton's sheltered perspective and Will's street knowledge

jokes.text: Your annual height check?
jokes.setup: Carlton asking what they always do first weekend after New Year
jokes.punchline: Will mockingly suggests Carlton's height measurement as an annual tradition
jokes.explanation: Classic height joke targeting Carlton's most obvious physical trait

jokes.text: Just go upstairs and wax your skis or your legs or whatever you wax.
jokes.setup: Will agreeing to go on the ski trip
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Carlton might wax his legs, playing on his prissy personality
jokes.explanation: Plays on Carlton's metrosexual tendencies with the leg waxing implication

jokes.text: Asking Miss Lisa for permission?
jokes.setup: Will making a phone call
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately recognizing Will is asking his girlfriend for permission despite Will's earlier denial
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry observation exposes Will's hypocrisy about not needing permission

jokes.text: First of all, Benson, I ain't gotta ask nobody for permission.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey suggesting Will is asking permission
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive denial immediately before doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for irony - Will's protestations make the subsequent permission-asking funnier

jokes.text: [CLUCKING]
jokes.setup: Will just finished asking Lisa for permission
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey making chicken sounds to mock Will's henpecked behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy beat - Geoffrey's chicken sounds are the ideal punctuation to Will's hypocrisy

jokes.text: No, you must have been watching one of the other black skiers.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on Will's bad skiing fall
jokes.punchline: Will deflecting by pointing out he's likely the only Black skier there
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about demographics at ski resorts, delivered with Will's trademark confidence

jokes.text: Normally, I would ditch you on that last one... but, uh, I'm gonna let you in on a little something.
jokes.setup: Will responding to Carlton's planned Barbara Walters viewing
jokes.punchline: Will about to reveal something embarrassing after seeming cool
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Barbara Walters confession, functional setup work

jokes.text: Ever since I was like 7, I had a mad crush on Barbara Walters.
jokes.setup: Will about to reveal something personal
jokes.punchline: Admitting a childhood crush on elderly news anchor Barbara Walters
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and specific confession that's both absurd and somehow believable for Will's character

jokes.text: I thought I was the only one.
jokes.setup: Will's Barbara Walters confession
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately revealing he shares this bizarre crush
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Carlton's immediate agreement makes both characters funnier and creates bonding moment

jokes.text: How do you feel about Nancy Reagan?
jokes.setup: Shared Barbara Walters confession
jokes.punchline: Carlton testing if their shared taste extends to another older political figure
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation that pushes the absurdity further while staying true to Carlton's political leanings

jokes.text: Oh, see now you're being just sick.
jokes.setup: Carlton's Nancy Reagan question
jokes.punchline: Will drawing the line at Nancy Reagan while being fine with Barbara Walters
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the escalation, Will's moral boundaries are hilariously arbitrary

jokes.text: Look, Miss Cobb, I know what you're up to and I'm not interested. You have eyes, take a look, I'm with him. This is our special weekend.
jokes.setup: Woman approaches Will and Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton completely misreading the situation and claiming Will as his romantic partner
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Carlton's possessiveness and social obliviousness create perfect misunderstanding

jokes.text: Wait a minute, baby. I don't even know this guy.
jokes.setup: Carlton just claimed Will as his romantic partner
jokes.punchline: Will immediately distancing himself from Carlton's misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Will's mortification makes Carlton's misunderstanding funnier

jokes.text: In fact, I'm going to go upstairs right now and have myself a good cry.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking if Geoffrey will miss her
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's obviously sarcastic response delivered with perfect deadpan
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey deadpan sarcasm that completely goes over Hilary's head

jokes.text: [CRIES]
jokes.setup: Geoffrey just said he'd go cry about Hilary leaving
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey actually following through with fake crying sounds
jokes.explanation: Escalates Geoffrey's sarcasm by actually performing the fake crying, perfect commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Wow, he's even more upset about my leaving than I thought.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey obviously fake crying
jokes.punchline: Hilary completely missing the sarcasm and taking it as genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary obliviousness - her complete misreading makes Geoffrey's sarcasm even funnier

jokes.text: Oh, Ashley, mi casa es your casa. You can come over any time you want.
jokes.setup: Ashley saying she'll miss Hilary
jokes.punchline: Hilary's seemingly generous offer about to be immediately contradicted
jokes.explanation: Sets up the immediate contradiction, functional setup work

jokes.text: Why don't you come over on Mondays between 11:15 and 2:30?
jokes.setup: Listing days Ashley can't visit
jokes.punchline: Narrowing down to absurdly specific time window
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - reduces 'anytime' to a hilariously narrow window with oddly specific times

jokes.text: Hilary, I'm in school.
jokes.setup: Hilary offering Monday 11:15-2:30 time slot
jokes.punchline: Ashley pointing out the obvious problem with weekday afternoon visits
jokes.explanation: Ashley's practical reality check highlights Hilary's thoughtless scheduling

jokes.text: Well, if you can't find the time...
jokes.setup: Ashley explaining she's in school during the offered time
jokes.punchline: Hilary acting like Ashley is being unreasonable about availability
jokes.explanation: Peak Hilary selfishness - turning Ashley's reasonable scheduling conflict into a guilt trip

jokes.text: I drink another cocoa, I mess around, I have to let out a Hershey bar.
jokes.setup: Carlton offering another cocoa
jokes.punchline: Will's crude euphemism about bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Mild bathroom humor with chocolate pun - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: The hills are alive.
jokes.setup: Carlton admiring the mountain scenery
jokes.punchline: Carlton quoting Sound of Music in earnest appreciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his earnest musical theater reference fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: get the tub started, let Calgon take you halfway, and I'll come take you the rest.
jokes.setup: Will telling Lisa to start a bath
jokes.punchline: Twisting the Calgon commercial slogan into a sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Clever reworking of a familiar advertising slogan into romantic suggestion

jokes.text: Carlton, this is not your car. Then get out of my rental car.
jokes.setup: Carlton telling Will to get out of his car
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately adjusting when corrected but maintaining the same demand
jokes.explanation: Carlton's petty insistence on authority even when technically incorrect

jokes.text: Now I'll never get to see that Barbara Walters Special. And she had Henry Winkler too. You made me miss Fonzie.
jokes.setup: Carlton upset about missing TV
jokes.punchline: Referencing the earlier Barbara Walters crush and adding Henry Winkler disappointment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that maintains Carlton's priorities even in crisis

jokes.text: I know, these air bags are worthless.
jokes.setup: Carlton expressing disbelief about their crash
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact complaint about car safety features after a major accident
jokes.explanation: Understated reaction to their dramatic situation, treating airbags like a consumer complaint

jokes.text: I swear, God as my witness... I will be in church first thing Sunday morning. Oh, wait, Sunday's bad. I'll cancel, I'll cancel.
jokes.setup: Will making desperate promises to God
jokes.punchline: Immediately negotiating with God about scheduling conflicts
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's street-smart pragmatism extends even to deathbed bargaining with God

jokes.text: Which reminds me, you still owe me 20 bucks from three years ago.
jokes.setup: Will mentioning his life flashing before his eyes
jokes.punchline: Using near-death experience as debt collection opportunity
jokes.explanation: Will's practical nature - even facing death, he's focused on collecting money owed

jokes.text: Back in Philly, they used to call me Spider-Man.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he can climb the icy cliff
jokes.punchline: Referencing his supposed Spider-Man nickname to justify the plan
jokes.explanation: Classic Will bravado - claiming heroic abilities with childhood nickname

jokes.text: So this is it. This is how the Carlton Banks story ends. With a whimper. [WHIMPERS]
jokes.setup: Carlton facing their dire situation
jokes.punchline: Carlton making a T.S. Eliot reference then literally whimpering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - literary pretension followed by literal whimpering. Great character integration

jokes.text: Hi-de-hi-de-Heidi-Fleiss.
jokes.setup: Will playing with echo making sounds
jokes.punchline: Turning Cab Calloway's 'hi-de-hi-de-ho' into Heidi Fleiss reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting jazz scatting to contemporary scandal figure

jokes.text: Oh. Sweetheart, you know expressions like 'when hell freezes over'? 'When the cows come home'? 'When donkeys fly'? Pick one.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking when she can move out
jokes.punchline: Phil listing multiple 'never' expressions and letting Ashley choose
jokes.explanation: Phil's parental humor - giving Ashley choices while making the same point

jokes.text: I've been trying to for, like, a half hour.
jokes.setup: Carlton telling Will to put the mirror down
jokes.punchline: Will revealing his hand is stuck to the mirror from the cold
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy revelation - adds absurd detail to their survival situation

jokes.text: Will, I want you to eat me. If I die first. You can stay alive by using my body for nourishment. Incidentally, the buttocks has the most meat.
jokes.setup: Carlton giving survival advice
jokes.punchline: Carlton matter-of-factly discussing cannibalism with anatomical specifics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Carlton's earnest, educational approach to discussing cannibalism

jokes.text: God, I hope I go first.
jokes.setup: Carlton's cannibalism suggestion
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified realization he'd rather die than eat Carlton
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Will's preference for death over Carlton consumption is both dark and funny

jokes.text: I didn't get the insurance. I put the car on my credit card... and I didn't get the $6 insurance.
jokes.setup: Carlton having violent spasms about something
jokes.punchline: Carlton's crisis is about car rental insurance, not impending death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton priorities - more upset about financial liability than mortality. Great character comedy

jokes.text: I don't know what's worse. Dying a slow agonizing death or staying alive and listening to you.
jokes.setup: Carlton obsessing over insurance costs
jokes.punchline: Will comparing death to listening to Carlton's complaints
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their friendship dynamics - Will's exasperation with Carlton transcends life-and-death situations

jokes.text: I don't have any friends.
jokes.setup: Carlton getting vulnerable in their situation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sad admission about his social life
jokes.explanation: Shifts tone to vulnerability while maintaining Carlton's social awkwardness

jokes.text: I don't do anything for you.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Will is different from other friends
jokes.punchline: Carlton realizing he offers Will nothing in return
jokes.explanation: Self-aware moment that's both funny and touching - Carlton recognizing the friendship imbalance

jokes.text: I just called you Thumbelina this morning.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Will doesn't make fun of him as much lately
jokes.punchline: Will citing a recent height insult as proof of continued mockery
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing Will's care through maintained friendly insults

jokes.text: Yeah, but your heart wasn't in it.
jokes.setup: Will citing his morning insult
jokes.punchline: Carlton noting that Will's insults lack their usual passion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation - Carlton can tell when Will's insults are halfhearted, showing their deep friendship

jokes.text: Stumpy.
jokes.setup: Will declaring Carlton his best friend
jokes.punchline: Will immediately following heartfelt declaration with affectionate insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will shows love through renewed passionate insults. Economical and touching

jokes.text: I'd cry, but my tear ducts are frozen.
jokes.setup: Carlton being moved by Will's friendship declaration
jokes.punchline: Carlton's inability to cry due to extreme cold
jokes.explanation: Maintains emotional moment while adding physical comedy about their dire situation

jokes.text: You'd let me jump like that?
jokes.setup: Carlton offering to jump down for help, Will immediately agreeing
jokes.punchline: Carlton's shock that Will would so readily let him risk his life
jokes.explanation: Carlton's naivety about Will's practical nature - expected heroic protest, got practical agreement

jokes.text: Why not? [YELLING THEN ECHOING] Why not? [RUMBLING] BOTH: Avalanche!
jokes.setup: Stranger warns them not to yell
jokes.punchline: Will immediately yelling loudly and causing an avalanche
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation - their situation goes from bad to catastrophic due to Will's defiance

jokes.text: Now I see how people could jump out of burning buildings. You couldn't figure it out before?
jokes.setup: Will reflecting on newfound bravery
jokes.punchline: Carlton's incredulous response to Will's obviousness
jokes.explanation: Carlton's practical response to Will's 'profound' realization about survival instincts

jokes.text: Hey, isn't that great? Now you and I can stay together and Lisa can stay with her.
jokes.setup: Angie offers her room to solve the accommodation problem
jokes.punchline: Will completely misunderstanding the sleeping arrangements
jokes.explanation: Will's obliviousness to romantic implications, prioritizing Carlton's feelings

jokes.text: How could you not take the insurance? It only costs an extra $6.
jokes.setup: Back to civilization, Will still processing their adventure
jokes.punchline: Will's priorities focus on Carlton's insurance mistake rather than their survival
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Will absorbed Carlton's practical concerns

jokes.text: Camp Gucciwanka? Oh, yeah, that's Apache for 'over-privileged.'
jokes.setup: Photo album showing Hilary at summer camp
jokes.punchline: Hilary's fake Apache translation revealing her oblivious privilege
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining luxury brand with Native American naming, perfectly capturing Hilary's privilege

jokes.text: Oh, goodbye, little lamp. No, I could use this.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying goodbye to household items
jokes.punchline: Immediately deciding to take the lamp after saying goodbye to it
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary materialism - even emotional goodbyes are interrupted by acquisitive instincts

jokes.text: One down, four to go. Take it easy, Philip.
jokes.setup: Phil celebrating Hilary's departure
jokes.punchline: Vivian correcting his math about remaining children and timeframe
jokes.explanation: Phil's parental exhaustion meets Vivian's practical reality check about empty nest timeline

jokes.text: No, three is a crowd. Come on. Okay.
jokes.setup: Will inviting Carlton to dinner with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Carlton refusing then immediately changing his mind
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's growth - from possessive to accepting their evolved friendship dynamic

jokes.text: I hate my life.
jokes.setup: Hilary realizes she forgot Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan response to being forgotten and left behind
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey ending - his existential despair at being treated as an afterthought is both funny and poignant
